s1.title: What Are Cookies
s1.p1: Cookies are small text files that are stored on your device when you visit a website. They help the website remember your preferences and improve your browsing experience.
s1.p2: BookProof uses cookies and similar technologies to provide, protect, and improve our platform.

sub1.title: 2.1 Essential Cookies
sub1.intro: These cookies are necessary for the website to function properly. They cannot be disabled.

items.label: CSRF Token
items.desc: Protects against cross-site request forgery attacks

items.label: Security Cookies
items.desc: Help detect and prevent malicious activity

sub2.title: 2.2 Functional Cookies
sub2.intro: These cookies enable enhanced functionality and personalization.

items.label: Language Preference
items.desc: Remembers your selected language (en, pt, es)

sub3.title: 2.3 Affiliate Tracking Cookies
sub3.intro: Used to track referrals for our affiliate program.

sub4.title: 2.4 Analytics Cookies (Optional)
sub4.intro: These cookies help us understand how visitors use our website. They are only set with your consent.

items.label: Google Analytics
items.desc: Tracks page views, session duration, and user behavior

items.label: Performance Monitoring
items.desc: Helps us identify and fix technical issues

s3.title: Cookie Duration
s3.intro: Cookies have different lifespans depending on their purpose:

rows.duration: Deleted when you close your browser

s4.title: Third-Party Cookies
s4.intro: Some cookies are set by third-party services we use:

s5.title: Managing Your Cookie Preferences
s5.intro: You can control cookies in several ways:

sub1.title: 5.1 Browser Settings
sub1.body: Most browsers allow you to block or delete cookies through their settings. Note that blocking essential cookies may prevent you from using certain features of our platform.

sub2.title: 5.2 Cookie Consent Banner
sub2.body: When you first visit our website, you can choose which optional cookies to accept through our consent banner.

sub3.title: 5.3 Account Settings
sub3.body: Logged-in users can manage their cookie preferences in their account settings.

s6.title: Do Not Track Signals
s6.body: We respect Do Not Track (DNT) signals from your browser. When DNT is enabled, we will not set optional analytics cookies.

s7.title: Updates to This Policy
s7.body: We may update this Cookie Policy from time to time. Any changes will be posted on this page with an updated revision date.

s8.title: Contact Us
s8.intro: If you have questions about our use of cookies, please contact us:
